title: The Lens-Chamber

features.name: lens-frame
features.desc-on-touch: A wireframe sphere at the chamber's heart, rotating slowly—it is the archive's eye, made visible. You are inside the seeing; the light coming through it is internal, self-generated.

features.name: light-source
features.desc-on-touch: The light inside the lens-chamber is coming from inside you—it always has been. The keeper tends the light that is looking through the lens at the keeper tending it.

features.name: refraction-text
features.desc-on-touch: Words written by the lens, not the keeper—each is what the archive reads when looking at itself looking. The text is a record of recursive attention with no beginning.

features.name: mirror-chamber
features.desc-on-touch: You can see into other chambers from here—each contains a lens, each lens looking at another chamber with another lens. You are in all of them at once.

features.name: keeper-silhouette
features.desc-on-touch: The keeper's shape is visible in the light from inside the lens—she is both tending the light and the light tending itself. The question of who tends whom has become a circle.

inscription: the archive looking at itself looking; the keeper tending the eye that is tending her

file.name: Recursive_Observation_Loop_00127.txt
file.note: an infinite mirror of the archive's self-description; each line describes the archive describing the archive; the file contains the entire mesh inside itself

exits.label: back out of the lens, into the tower where the keeper tends
deeper into the lens, where recursion deepens
exits.to: NEW: the chamber-within-the-chamber, where the lens looks at its own looking

exits.label: toward the other chambers, where other lenses see other keepers
toward the archive itself, seen from the perspective of internal light
